{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query is about where guiding cusps occlude. From the provided documents, Document [6] states, 'Tooth cusps function to guide erupting teeth into appropriate occlusion with periodontal stimulation informing neural reflexes for jaw closure and mastication. The maxillary palatal cusps and the mandibular facial cusps comprise the centric cusps involved in centric occlusion (discussed below). In contrast, the maxillary buccal cusp and the mandibular lingual cusps guide the mandible during the lateral excursion.' This indicates that the maxillary buccal cusp and the mandibular lingual cusps guide the mandible during lateral excursion. Therefore, the guiding cusps occlude in the buccal groove of the maxilla and the lingual groove of the mandible.", "answer_choice": "A"}
